items.title: Intel's upcoming Xeon 7 "Diamond Rapids" server CPUs reportedly delayed to 2027 — Next-gen Coral Rapids lineup lands 2028 but can be accelerated, according to new leak
items.description: Diamond Rapids, also known as Xeon 7, was supposed to launch later this year, but a new leak from Jaykihn claims the timeline has now moved to 2027. Clearwater Forest, the E-core-only successor to Sierra Forest, is set to release in the first half of this year, while the next-gen Coral Rapids family is reportedly planned for 2028.

Subscribe to our newsletter Last year, Intel canceled the 8-channel variant of its upcoming Diamond Rapids server lineup, choosing instead to focus on only the 16-channel SKUs. The "Xeon 7" family was originally supposed to launch later this year, but new information from leaker Jaykihn suggests it's been pushed back to 2027. That means Diamond Rapids won't be able to compete directly with AMD's EPYC Venice CPUs that're (still) slated for 2026.The leak also says, at launch, the Diamond Rapids family will top out at 256 cores (all P-cores), but 512-core silicon will follow a few months later, both featuring 16-channel memory. That means up to 1.6 TB/s of throughput thanks to MRDIMM 2 support. The P-cores inside Diamond Rapids will be using the "Panther Cove-X" architecture, and both the 256- and 512-core lineups are rumored to be compatible with the LGA9324 socket.Diamond Rapids will be the last Xeon generation without hyperthreading support, as the next-gen Coral Rapids lineup is said to bring back SMT. Speaking of which, Coral Rapids is apparently planned for a mid-2028 launch and will begin with 8-channel variants. However, the rollout can be called up and accelerated in response to market demand, as stated in Intel's recent quarterly earnings call. Beyond that, details on Coral Rapids and its specs are currently nonexistent.Article continues below You may like AMD Zen 6 and Intel Nova Lake CPUs reportedly arriving late, delayed to CES 2027 Intel's roadmaps examined — 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push Intel returns to boxed workstation CPUs with Xeon 600 — Granite Rapids WS delivers up to 86 cores, 4TB of memory, and 128 PCIe 5 lanes Snapshot, subject to change.Clearwater Forest 1H 2026.Diamond Rapids mid 2027, 16CH.Coral Rapids mid 2028, starting with 8CH.As mentioned in Q1 call, may be accelerated.Crescent Island and Crescent Island Workstation late 2026, Xe3p.Jaguar Shores late 2027, Xe4.April 24, 2026Lastly, the post above claims Clearwater Forest is due in the first half of 2026. As a reminder, Clearwater Forest is the successor to the existing Sierra Forrest lineup, which features only E-cores, but with major upgrades. It's classified as the Xeon 6+ platform, meant to bridge the gap between Xeon 6 and Xeon 7 (Diamond Rapids). As such, it's manufactured on the Intel 18A node and features up to 288 Darkmont E-cores. It supports 12-channel DDR5-8000 memory.Most of this information isn't official, so exercise caution before taking it at face value. With Intel's roadmaps being as unclear as they are, a lot can change between now and even the second half of the year when Diamond Rapids was originally supposed to launch. In contrast, AMD's EPYC Venice and Verano lineups seem remarkably stable, with the former on track for launch later this year and the latter in 2027.You'll notice we didn't talk about the Arc Xe graphics mentioned in the leak — that's because we've already covered that in detail in another post. With all the focus on data centers and AI, consumer products such as dedicated gaming GPUs keep getting sidelined, but at least the CPU side of things for the mainstream segment isn't as affected. Nonetheless, it's shaping up to be a fierce server battle between the Red and Blue Teams in the coming years. items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 英特尔即将推出的Xeon 7“Diamond Rapids”服务器CPU据称推迟至2027年——下一代Coral Rapids系列将于2028年问世，但据新泄露消息，可加快进度
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔最新的服务器CPU路线图出现重大调整。原计划今年晚些时候推出的Diamond Rapids（钻石激流）家族中，8通道内存版本已被取消，仅保留16通道SKU，且整体发布时间推迟至2027年。这意味着英特尔将无法用该系列直接对抗AMD计划于2026年推出的EPYC Venice处理器。

根据爆料者Jaykihn的信息，Diamond Rapids首发将提供256个核心（全部为性能核，即P核），采用“Panther Cove-X”架构，随后几个月内还将推出512核版本。两者均支持16通道内存，借助MRDIMM 2技术可实现高达1.6 TB/s的内存带宽。这两款产品均兼容新的LGA9324插槽。值得注意的是，Diamond Rapids将是英特尔最后一代不支持超线程的至强处理器——其继任者Coral Rapids（珊瑚激流）将重新引入同步多线程（SMT）技术。

Coral Rapids计划于2028年中期推出，初期提供8通道内存版本。英特尔在最近的季度财报电话会议中表示，可根据市场需求加速或提前该产品的上市节奏。目前尚缺乏关于Coral Rapids的具体规格细节。

在Diamond Rapids之前，英特尔还将推出Clearwater Forest（清水森林），作为现有Sierra Forest（仅含能效核E核）的继任者，定位为Xeon 6+平台，旨在填补Xeon 6与Xeon 7（Diamond Rapids）之间的空白。它采用Intel 18A工艺制造，配备最多288个Darkmont E核，支持12通道DDR5-8000内存，预计于2026年上半年发布。

此外，泄露信息还提到了Crescent Island（新月岛）及Crescent Island工作站版本（2026年底，Xe3p架构）、Jaguar Shores（美洲虎海岸，2027年底，Xe4架构）等AI加速器产品的规划。

需要指出的是，上述大部分信息并非官方确认，英特尔当下的路线图仍存在较大不确定性，从目前到原定发布窗口（2026年下半年）之间可能发生诸多变化。相比之下，AMD的EPYC Venice（计划2026年）和Verano（计划2027年）产品线显得更为稳定。这场红蓝两队在服务器市场的竞争将在未来几年愈发激烈。

items.title: Maine governor vetoes bill that bans large new data centers — says legislature should’ve exempted one particular well-supported data center
items.description: Governor Janet Mills vetoed a bill that would have enacted a moratorium on all data center projects because it would affect one site to be built on a former paper mill in Jay, Franklin County.

Subscribe to our newsletter Maine Governor Janet Mills just vetoed a bill that would ban all new data center projects in the state. This moratorium applies to all data centers that exceed 20MW of consumption and would have halted all approvals until October 2027, while a state council studies their impact on the local power and water supply, as well as their effect on surrounding air quality. According to Bangor Daily News, the governor said she supported the temporary moratorium on this power-hungry infrastructure if the legislature exempted a site that’s already under construction in Jay, a town in Franklin County that sits around 60 miles north of Portland.“A moratorium is appropriate given the impacts of massive data centers in other states on the environment and on electricity rates,” the Democrat governor said in a statement. “But the final version of this bill fails to allow for a specific project in [Jay] that enjoys strong local support from its host community and region.”The site where the Jay data center is being built was once a paper mill that suffered a catastrophic accident, leading to its closure in 2023. It’s reported that the $550 million project will use existing infrastructure, limiting its impact on the electric grid and energy bills, while also contributing to Jay’s property tax revenue. Furthermore, it’s expected to create 800 construction jobs and open 100 additional high-paying permanent positions once the data center begins operations.Article continues below You may like Virginia voter support for new data centers collapses from 69% in 2023 to 35% in new poll Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center It’s reported that the Democratic governor has never had a veto overturned by the legislature, which is led by the same party. However, the Maine House of Representatives voted against the exemption, 115-29, which is more than enough to meet the two-thirds majority required to override Mills. Aside from disagreeing with the majority of the Maine Democratic Party, this also exposes her to a political challenge as she attempts to secure a nomination as the Democratic contender for a U.S. Senate seat, facing political newcomer Graham Platner.Despite her move to block the bill, Mills said she will issue an executive order to create a state council to study the impact of data center projects in the state. She also signed a bill that put into law a measure preventing data center projects from taking advantage of the state’s business development tax incentive programs.Residents and local governments have started pushing back against data center projects after many communities with nearby infrastructure have reported increased electricity costs and poor power quality. It has gotten so bad that President Donald Trump convened some of the biggest hyperscalers at the White House and made them promise to “pay their own way” for their electricity needs. items.titleZh: 缅因州州长否决禁止新建大型数据中心的法案——称立法机构本应豁免一个特别受支持的数据中心
items.descriptionZh:
缅因州州长珍妮特·米尔斯（Janet Mills）近日否决了一项旨在暂停该州所有新建大型数据中心项目的法案。该法案原本计划对功耗超过20兆瓦的数据中心实施临时禁令，直至2027年10月，期间由州议会委员会研究这些设施对当地电力供应、水资源以及空气质量的影响。然而，米尔斯州长在否决声中提出了一个关键条件：她支持这项针对高能耗基础设施的临时禁令，但前提是立法机构必须豁免一个已经在富兰克林县杰伊镇（Jay）开工建设的项目——该镇位于波特兰以北约60英里处，原址曾是一家纸厂，2023年因重大事故关闭。

米尔斯在声明中解释了她的立场：“鉴于其他州大型数据中心对环境和电价的冲击，暂停是合理的。但该法案的最终版本未能为一个在杰伊镇获得当地社区和地区强烈支持的具体项目留出豁免空间。”据报道，这个耗资5.5亿美元的杰伊数据中心项目将利用现有基础设施，从而减少对电网和能源账单的负担，同时还能为杰伊镇增加房产税收入。此外，项目建设期间预计创造800个建筑岗位，运营后将开放100个高薪永久职位。

这一政治博弈的背景颇为复杂。米尔斯作为民主党籍州长，此前从未被同样由民主党主导的立法机构推翻过否决。然而，这次缅因州众议院以115票对29票的压倒性结果否决了豁免条款，这一票数远超推翻州长否决所需的三分之二多数。这意味着，如果立法机构后续正式投票推翻州长对整个法案的否决，米尔斯将面临政治尴尬。更关键的是，她正在争取民主党联邦参议员提名，而她的党内初选对手、政治新人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（Graham Platner）正可以利用这一分歧发起挑战。

尽管否决了法案，米尔斯表示将发布行政令，成立一个州级委员会研究数据中心项目的影响。她还签署了另一项法案，正式禁止数据中心项目利用该州的商业发展税收激励计划。这一系列动作反映出数据中心扩张引发的社会矛盾日益尖锐：许多社区报告称，附近大型数据中心导致居民电价上涨、供电质量恶化，民众和地方政府开始强烈抵制。甚至总统唐纳德·特朗普也曾召集几家最大的超大规模云服务商在白宫会面，要求他们必须“自掏腰包”解决电力需求问题。

items.title: Intel has reportedly cancelled discrete gaming GPUs for the upcoming Xe3P Arc "Celestial" family — gaming GPU remains uncertain even for the next-gen Xe4 "Druid" lineup that lands in 2027
items.description: New leaks claim that Intel's upcoming Xe3P graphics architecture won't feature any discrete gaming GPUs, and even the next-gen Xe4 lineup isn't confirmed to. Intel is instead prioritizing the datacenter and workstation segments for new graphics IP, and is featuring them on mobile parts.

Subscribe to our newsletter Panther Lake debuted Intel's latest Xe3 graphics architecture, but the IP isn't said to expand beyond iGPUs. For a while, we've known that "Xe3P" (codenamed Celestial) will serve as the refined follow-up, with many awaiting a proper successor to the existing Arc B-series dedicated GPUs. Unfortunately, a massive new leak dump from reliable tipster Jaykihn claims Intel canceled discrete gaming GPUs for Celestial a long time ago, and their fate hangs in the balance for even the next-gen Xe4 "Druid" architecture.Celestial was canned long ago.Druid is up in the air.April 24, 2026Originally, Xe3 was supposed to be Celestial with a planned 2025 launch, but Intel changed it to Battlemage, pushing Xe3P to be Celestial instead. That's why the iGPUs inside Panther Lake chips are classified as Battlemage parts. Last year, Intel announced the Crescent Island data center GPU as the first Xe3P/Celestial product, featuring an unprecedented 160 GB of LPDDR5X VRAM, slated for a 2026 release (that Jaykihn confirms for late 2026). The updated Intel Arc roadmap (Xe3 announced in 2025 but launched in 2026) (Image credit: Intel)Xe3P is also reportedly used across Nova Lake for the display and media engine block, while a special NVL-S desktop CPU featuring 12 Xe3P cores (iGPU) is also in the works; NVL-S otherwise features only 2 Xe cores. Nova Lake-H (mobile) is rumored to use Xe3P throughout the entire lineup. So that, mixed with Cresent Island, perhaps Arc Pro-series workstation GPUs, and the rumored "Razor Lake-AX" (Strix Halo competitor after Nova Lake-AX was canned), constitutes the entirety of Xe3P.Article continues below You may like Intel's Nova Lake lineup to feature a SKU with 12 Xe3P iGPU cores, compete with Ryzen G-series APUs Panther Lake for handhelds reportedly delayed into Q2 2026, Core G3 series will tackle AMD's Ryzen Z2 Intel's upcoming Xeon 7 "Diamond Rapids" server CPUs reportedly delayed to 2027 There is no discrete gaming-focused graphics card planned. That means Battlemage will remain Intel's latest gaming dGPU at least until the next-gen Xe4/Druid architecture comes along. Jaykihn's leak points to a late-2027 release for Druid, but the possibility of a discrete gaming GPU remains "up in the air." Intel is planning to focus on the AI sector once again, with "Jaguar Shores" marking the debut of Xe4.Snapshot, subject to change.Clearwater Forest 1H 2026.Diamond Rapids mid 2027, 16CH.Coral Rapids mid 2028, starting with 8CH.As mentioned in Q1 call, may be accelerated.Crescent Island and Crescent Island Workstation late 2026, Xe3p.Jaguar Shores late 2027, Xe4.April 24, 2026Around the same time, the "Titan Lake" mainstream consumer CPUs might also feature Xe4 IP for integrated graphics and/or other display blocks. A supposed "Arc D-series" for gamers yearning for a next-gen dedicated GPU from Intel remains unconfirmed. The successor to Xe4 is rumored to be planned for mid-to-late 2028, according to the leak, and is likely to debut on whatever comes after Jaguar Shores in the rack-scale segment.Xe3P = Celestial.Followed by Xe4 down the line.Xe4 successor may or may not be Xe5.Intel’s graphical architecture naming is not as straightforward as integer iterations; Xe4 itself has had multiple names, and multiple architectures have been referred to as Xe4, over time.April 24, 2026All of this information is, of course, subject to change and of a speculative nature. Intel's roadmaps haven't been the most consistent, so take these leaks with a grain of salt. The main takeaway is how Intel has almost given up on the discrete gaming GPU market after a turbulent launch for Alchemist and Battlemage. Given the AI boom we're in, it's easy to understand how Celestial production could've been entirely reallocated to serve the datacenter and workstation markets. Let's hope that Druid marks the revival of dedicated gaming GPUs from the Blue Team. items.titleZh: 据报道，英特尔已取消即将推出的Xe3P Arc "Celestial"系列独立游戏GPU — 即使是2027年登场的下一代Xe4 "Druid"系列，游戏GPU仍存变数
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔最新GPU路线图曝光：独立游戏显卡计划搁浅，重心转向AI与数据中心

近日，可靠爆料人Jaykihn泄露了英特尔GPU架构的详细路线图，揭示了代号为“Celestial”（Xe3P）和“Druid”（Xe4）的后续发展计划。消息称，英特尔已长期取消Celestial架构的独立游戏显卡项目，下一代Xe4“Druid”架构的独立游戏显卡命运同样悬而未决。

背景与架构演变

此前已知，Panther Lake处理器首次集成了英特尔最新的Xe3图形架构，但该IP仅限于集成GPU（iGPU），并未扩展至独立显卡。随后，代号“Celestial”的Xe3P架构被视为Xe3的改进版本，原本被寄望于成为现有Arc B系列独立游戏显卡的继任者。然而，根据最新泄露信息，英特尔在很久以前就砍掉了Celestial的独立游戏GPU计划。

架构命名上存在一定混淆：原计划中Xe3应是Celestial，预计2025年发布，但英特尔将其改为Battlemage（即当前Arc B系列），而Xe3P则成为Celestial。这也是为什么Panther Lake芯片中的iGPU被归类为Battlemage部件的原因。

Xe3P/Celestial的实际用途

虽然独立游戏显卡被取消，但Xe3P并未消失。英特尔已于去年宣布了Crescent Island数据中心GPU作为首个Xe3P/Celestial产品，配备前所未有的160GB LPDDR5X显存，计划于2026年下半年发布（Jaykihn确认时间为2026年底）。此外，Xe3P还将用于Nova Lake的显示与媒体引擎模块，一款配备12个Xe3P核心（iGPU）的NVL-S桌面CPU也在开发中（其他NVL-S型号仅2个Xe核心）。移动版Nova Lake-H则预计全系采用Xe3P。加上Crescent Island、可能的Arc Pro系列工作站显卡，以及传闻中取代被砍的Nova Lake-AX的“Razor Lake-AX”（对标Strix Halo），这些构成了Xe3P的全部阵容——没有任何面向游戏玩家的独立显卡产品。

Xe4/Druid的未来

这意味着Battlemage将至少在2027年底前保持英特尔最新的游戏独立GPU地位。Jaykihn的泄露显示，Xe4/Druid架构预计于2027年底发布，但独立游戏显卡的可能性仍“悬而未决”。英特尔正再次将重点转向AI领域，首个Xe4产品将是名为“Jaguar Shores”的数据中心/机架级GPU。同时，主流消费级CPU“Titan Lake”也可能集成Xe4用于iGPU或显示模块。而传说中的“Arc D系列”独立游戏显卡仍未得到确认。

泄露的后续路线图还包括：Xe4的继任者（可能叫Xe5或其它名称）计划于2028年中后期推出，预计首发于Jaguar Shores之后的机架级产品。

性能与市场影响

英特尔图形架构的命名并非简单的整数迭代，Xe4本身曾有过多个名称，多个架构也一度被统称为Xe4。此次泄露信息虽属推测且可能变动，但核心信号明确：在经历了Alchemist和Battlemage的波折后，英特尔几乎放弃了独立游戏GPU市场。在当前AI热潮下，不难理解为何Celestial的生产资源被完全重新分配给数据中心和工作站市场。对于玩家而言，希望或许只能寄托于Druid架构能带来蓝色阵营独立游戏显卡的复兴。

总结来看，英特尔GPU路线图已清晰转向：独立游戏显卡短期内不再有新品，未来数年重心将放在集成GPU、数据中心AI加速与工作站领域。Druid是否能成为游戏玩家期待的转折点，尚需时间验证。

items.title: Intel VP claims up to 30% of CPU performance is untapped by modern games — software optimization is critical to unlocking full potential of hybrid CPUs
items.description: Intel is claiming that better software optimization for hybrid architecture CPUs can unlock up to 30% more performance without hardware upgrades. The silicon itself is very capable, but its true potential is held back by code that perhaps prioritizes more conventional silicon and, therefore, benefits from brute-force upgrades like extra L3 cache.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel switched to a hybrid architecture for its CPUs back in 2021 with Alder Lake, mixing performance and efficiency cores on the same package, similar to ARM-based chips. Since then, the company has iterated on the design slowly but steadily. And even though a "unified core" is in the works, for now, the hybrid architecture seems to have reached maturity from a hardware standpoint, according to Intel VP Robert Hallock.Hallock sat down with PC Games Hardware and, during the interview, blamed the software side of things for performance issues on hybrid chips. He was referring to the practice of disabling E-cores on modern Intel CPUs to get better performance; some people report higher FPS in games when playing only on the P-cores. Hallock's response to this was that "they are virtually identical in performance… it’s about 1% difference."Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptHe went on to explain how the early state of the Intel Thread Director back in the day contributed to better P-core-only performance. Windows' task scheduler is essentially blind without the Thread Director actually telling it which process is better suited to which core (hence the name "director"). With better optimization, even though the E-cores aren't as powerful, they still contribute their fair share to the overall operation. Even simple tools like Intel's APO can help in this regard.Article continues below You may like Intel’s Binary Optimization Tool tested and explained Geekbench investigates up to 30% jump with Intel's iBOT Intel announces Arrow Lake Refresh CPUs, claims 15% higher gaming performance and multi-threaded boost Beyond that, though, there were also issues with low ring bus frequencies with E-cores at the time that contributed to worse performance. With them enabled, even if your P-cores were ready to boost much higher, the interconnect would be bottlenecked to lower speeds because the E-cores on the same die just couldn't keep up. Intel has worked to more clearly decouple the core clusters in subsequent generations, like Raptor and Arrow Lake. Arrow Lake 200S Die Shot (Image credit: Tony Yu from Asus, via Bilibili)Hallock continued, saying that he truly believes "that the general PC gaming market and especially enthusiasts [...] are significantly underestimating the importance of software to the PC experience." Software optimization is the next frontier of efficiency, of extracting more performance from the same silicon, because the silicon itself is seemingly not the bottleneck.Things like the new binary optimization feature Intel has packed inside the Arrow Lake refresh chips are an example of this. Even though it doesn't work for most apps and games yet — Geekbench even flagged it — it's proof that tuning code can lead to better performance on the same hardware. Beyond the program, everything from the driver to even the BIOS adds its own overhead, leaving performance on the table."Yes, you can make the game faster with a faster piece of hardware, but there's always going to be 10, 20, 30% performance hidden behind the fact that that game was just not optimized for your CPU," claimed Hallock. AMD's solution to this problem has been rather simple: just add a lot of SRAM next to the cores, aka 3D V-cache, so that the CPU's L3 cache needs are met quickly, helping achieve higher FPS in games.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsNova Lake has something similar in the works with its bLLC (Big Last Level Cache), but that's still a hardware solution. The thought of up to 30% performance just waiting to be extracted through better software optimization is therefore not overstated. In a way, Hallock is pointing fingers at developers and engineers who have optimized for AMD's relatively conventional silicon first, which affects the true potential of Intel's hybrid architecture. items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 英特尔副总裁声称现代游戏未能发掘高达30%的CPU性能——软件优化对于释放混合CPU的全部潜力至关重要
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔自2021年推出Alder Lake处理器以来，一直采用类似ARM芯片的混合架构，在同一芯片上集成高性能核心（P核）与高能效核心（E核）。此后，该公司对这一设计进行了稳步迭代。尽管“统一核心”正在研发中，但英特尔副总裁Robert Hallock在接受PC Games Hardware采访时表示，从硬件角度看，混合架构已达到成熟阶段，问题主要出在软件层面。

针对部分用户为了提升游戏性能而关闭E核的做法（即仅使用P核运行游戏），Hallock回应称，实际性能差异微乎其微，大约只有1%。他解释，早期英特尔的Thread Director（线程调度器）技术尚不成熟，导致Windows任务调度器无法准确判断哪个线程适合运行在哪种核心上，从而使得P核独占模式一度表现更好。但随着优化深入，E核虽然性能较弱，但仍能对整体运行贡献力量，甚至像Intel APO（二进制优化工具）这样简单的软件也能显著改善调度效率。

除了调度问题，Hallock还指出了早期混合架构的另一个瓶颈：环形总线频率。当E核启用时，即使P核可以超频到更高频率，但由于E核无法跟上，互联总线会被迫降低速度，从而限制整体性能。英特尔在后续的Raptor Lake和Arrow Lake等世代中，通过更清晰地解耦不同核心集群来缓解这一问题。

Hallock强调，PC游戏市场尤其是发烧友群体“严重低估了软件对PC体验的重要性”。他认为软件优化是提取更多性能的新前沿，因为硅片本身似乎不再是瓶颈。例如，英特尔在Arrow Lake Refresh芯片中集成的全新二进制优化功能，尽管目前尚未支持大多数应用和游戏（Geekbench甚至因此发出警告），但它证明了通过调整代码可以在同款硬件上获得更好的性能。从驱动程序到BIOS，每一层软件都会产生开销，导致性能损失。Hallock指出：“用更快的硬件可以让游戏运行更快，但总有10%、20%、30%的性能隐藏在那款游戏没有针对你的CPU进行优化这一事实上。”

AMD对此问题的解决方案相对直接：在核心旁增加大量SRAM（即3D V-Cache），快速满足CPU的L3缓存需求，从而在游戏中实现更高帧率。而英特尔未来的Nova Lake也计划引入类似的大末级缓存（bLLC），但这仍然是硬件方案。因此，通过更好的软件优化最多可释放30%性能的说法并非夸大其词。从某种意义上说，Hallock是在批评那些优先针对AMD相对传统的硅片进行优化的开发者和工程师，这限制了英特尔混合架构的真正潜力。

items.title: AMD's memory-boosting EXPO 1.2 is here, adds support for three Chinese memory vendors — performance gains could be muted until Zen 6
items.description: Third-party AMD developer and hardware leakers share details of the new AMD EXPO 1.2 technology arriving on AMD AM5 motherboards.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter AMD's long-awaited EXPO 1.2 has finally arrived on AM5 motherboards. The new and improved memory overclocking technology will allow vendors to release fast memory kits for AMD that rival the best RAM. Renowned third-party AMD utility developer 1usmus and hardware leaker chi11eddog have detailed the improvements in EXPO 1.2. However, according to the former, the most substantial benefit will likely be unlocked only with AMD's next-generation Zen 6 processors. The new version also adds support for three Chinese RAM vendors.One of AMD EXPO 1.2's enhancements appears to be support for module geometry. While 1usmus didn’t provide extensive details, module geometry refers to the arrangement and structure of memory chips on a memory module. The feature may be related to HUDIMMs, a recent innovation that suppresses one of the 32-bit sub-channels in DDR5 memory modules, aiming to offer a cost-effective solution to the ongoing memory shortage.AMD EXPO 1.2 introduces support for MRDIMMs (Multiplexed Rank Dual In-line Memory Modules), designed to deliver substantially higher bandwidth and greater memory capacity. These memory modules are for server and data center platforms, though, so they won't be available on consumer AM5 platforms.Article continues below You may like AMD's next-gen 'Medusa Halo' APUs to reportedly use LPDDR6 RAM, enabling 80% higher bandwidth AMD addressing crushing memory shortages with Ryzen 7 9850X3D CPU bundles China’s CXMT and YMTC to memory output Sadly, EXPO 1.2 still only partially supports CUDIMMs (Clocked Unbuffered Dual In-line Memory Modules) and CSODIMMs (Clocked Small Outline Dual In-line Memory Modules). These modules leverage a CKD (Client Clock Driver), a small IC that maintains signal integrity and stabilizes memory operation at higher frequencies. It's a key component to help DDR5 continue to scale the frequency ladder.However, EXPO 1.2 retains bypass mode for CUDIMMs, treating them as standard memory modules without fully leveraging the CKD's benefits. There is mention of CKD bypass modes, but we don't yet have any detailed information on them. According to 1usmus, the current AGESA 1.3.0.1 firmware does not yet fully support CUDIMMs. According to 1usmus, AMD is likely laying the groundwork for full CUDIMM support in preparation for the upcoming Ryzen 10000 processors (codenamed Olympic Ridge) that will leverage AMD's new Zen 6 execution cores.$4.99 thrift store GPU from r/pcmasterraceAMD has been making significant behind-the-scenes improvements on EXPO 1.2. One of the standout features introduced is the new Ultra Low Latency (ULL) mode. It reportedly reduces memory latency. Hardware leaker chi11eddog claims that enabling ULL mode can decrease memory latency by an impressive five to seven nanoseconds on a typical DDR5-6000 memory kit.Other improvements that EXPO 1.2 brings to the table are new fields for further fine-tuning memory that overclocking enthusiasts will appreciate. The new memory timings include tREFI, tRRDS, and tWR, along with options for VDDP voltage.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIn response to the ongoing global memory crisis, AMD has proactively expanded memory compatibility by adding support for three Chinese memory manufacturers: RAMXEED Limited Conexant, Rui Xuan (formerly known as Rei Zuan), and Fujitsu Synaptics. These brands are popular in China, but it's unlikely you'll see their memory kits in the Western markets. However, consumers who purchase Chinese memory kits from overseas via platforms such as AliExpress can expect full compatibility thanks to AMD EXPO 1.2.Asus has already started deploying beta firmware with early support for AMD EXPO 1.2 across its 800-series motherboard lineup. Many of the company's X870E and X870 motherboards can access the new firmware; however, the B850 motherboards still have to wait a little while longer. Other motherboard manufacturers will likely follow suit very soon.AMD EXPO 1.2 is a great sign of commitment to continue improving the AM5 platform. However, we won't get to experience its full potential until Zen 6 hits the market, which should be sometime between late 2026 and early 2027. items.author: Zhiye Liu
items.titleZh: AMD的内存提升技术EXPO 1.2到来，新增支持三家中国内存厂商——性能提升可能要到Zen 6才会显现
items.descriptionZh:
AMD 长期备受期待的 EXPO 1.2 内存超频技术终于登陆 AM5 主板，这一升级将为 AMD 平台带来与顶级内存相媲美的高速内存套件。知名第三方 AMD 工具开发者 1usmus 和硬件爆料者 chi11eddog 详细披露了 EXPO 1.2 的多项改进。然而，据 1usmus 透露，该技术最重大的优势可能需要等到 AMD 下一代 Zen 6 处理器（也就是 Ryzen 10000 系列，代号 Olympic Ridge）问世后才能完全解锁，预计时间在 2026 年底至 2027 年初之间。

在核心技术原理方面，EXPO 1.2 新增了对“模块几何”（module geometry）的支持，这指的是内存芯片在内存模块上的排列与结构。该特性可能与 HUDIMMs 有关——这是近期的一项创新，通过抑制 DDR5 内存模块中两个 32 位子通道之一，旨在以更低成本缓解内存短缺问题。此外，EXPO 1.2 还引入了对 MRDIMMs（多路复用秩双列内存模块）的支持，这类模块专为服务器和数据中心平台设计，可提供大幅提升的带宽和更大容量，但不会用于消费级 AM5 平台。

对于面向消费市场的 CUDIMMs（带时钟驱动的无缓冲双列直插式内存模块）和 CSODIMMs（带时钟驱动的小外形双列直插式内存模块），EXPO 1.2 仅提供部分支持。这些模块依赖 CKD（客户端时钟驱动器）——一种小型集成电路，用于保持信号完整性并稳定高频内存运行。但 EXPO 1.2 仍保留了 CUDIMMs 的旁路模式，将其作为标准内存模块处理，未能充分利用 CKD 的优势。当前 AGESA 1.3.0.1 固件尚未完全支持 CUDIMMs，AMD 可能正在为未来 Zen 6 处理器提供完整支持做准备。

性能参数方面，EXPO 1.2 引入了一项突出特性——全新的超低延迟（ULL）模式。据 chi11eddog 透露，在典型的 DDR5-6000 内存套件上启用 ULL 模式可将内存延迟降低惊人的 5 至 7 纳秒。此外，该版本还新增了多个内存时序微调选项，包括 tREFI、tRRDS 和 tWR，以及 VDDP 电压调节项，这无疑会深受超频爱好者的欢迎。

在技术影响和应用场景上，为应对全球内存危机，AMD 主动扩展了内存兼容性，新增了对三家中国内存厂商的支持：RAMXEED Limited Conexant、Rui Xuan（原 Rei Zuan）和 Fujitsu Synaptics。这些品牌在中国市场颇受欢迎，但在西方市场可能难以见到。不过，消费者通过海外平台（如 AliExpress）购买的中国内存套件将获得充分的 EXPO 1.2 兼容性。华硕已率先在其 800 系列主板（包括 X870E 和 X870）上部署了支持 EXPO 1.2 的测试版固件，B850 主板仍需等待，其他主板厂商预计很快跟进。总体而言，AMD EXPO 1.2 彰显了 AMD 持续改进 AM5 平台的承诺，尽管其全部潜力要等到 Zen 6 处理器发布后才能释放。

history.title: Intel's upcoming Xeon 7 "Diamond Rapids" server CPUs reportedly delayed to 2027 — Next-gen Coral Rapids lineup lands 2028 but can be accelerated, according to new leak
history.description: Diamond Rapids, also known as Xeon 7, was supposed to launch later this year, but a new leak from Jaykihn claims the timeline has now moved to 2027. Clearwater Forest, the E-core-only successor to Sierra Forest, is set to release in the first half of this year, while the next-gen Coral Rapids family is reportedly planned for 2028.

Subscribe to our newsletter Last year, Intel canceled the 8-channel variant of its upcoming Diamond Rapids server lineup, choosing instead to focus on only the 16-channel SKUs. The "Xeon 7" family was originally supposed to launch later this year, but new information from leaker Jaykihn suggests it's been pushed back to 2027. That means Diamond Rapids won't be able to compete directly with AMD's EPYC Venice CPUs that're (still) slated for 2026.The leak also says, at launch, the Diamond Rapids family will top out at 256 cores (all P-cores), but 512-core silicon will follow a few months later, both featuring 16-channel memory. That means up to 1.6 TB/s of throughput thanks to MRDIMM 2 support. The P-cores inside Diamond Rapids will be using the "Panther Cove-X" architecture, and both the 256- and 512-core lineups are rumored to be compatible with the LGA9324 socket.Diamond Rapids will be the last Xeon generation without hyperthreading support, as the next-gen Coral Rapids lineup is said to bring back SMT. Speaking of which, Coral Rapids is apparently planned for a mid-2028 launch and will begin with 8-channel variants. However, the rollout can be called up and accelerated in response to market demand, as stated in Intel's recent quarterly earnings call. Beyond that, details on Coral Rapids and its specs are currently nonexistent.Article continues below You may like AMD Zen 6 and Intel Nova Lake CPUs reportedly arriving late, delayed to CES 2027 Intel's roadmaps examined — 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push Intel returns to boxed workstation CPUs with Xeon 600 — Granite Rapids WS delivers up to 86 cores, 4TB of memory, and 128 PCIe 5 lanes Snapshot, subject to change.Clearwater Forest 1H 2026.Diamond Rapids mid 2027, 16CH.Coral Rapids mid 2028, starting with 8CH.As mentioned in Q1 call, may be accelerated.Crescent Island and Crescent Island Workstation late 2026, Xe3p.Jaguar Shores late 2027, Xe4.April 24, 2026Lastly, the post above claims Clearwater Forest is due in the first half of 2026. As a reminder, Clearwater Forest is the successor to the existing Sierra Forrest lineup, which features only E-cores, but with major upgrades. It's classified as the Xeon 6+ platform, meant to bridge the gap between Xeon 6 and Xeon 7 (Diamond Rapids). As such, it's manufactured on the Intel 18A node and features up to 288 Darkmont E-cores. It supports 12-channel DDR5-8000 memory.Most of this information isn't official, so exercise caution before taking it at face value. With Intel's roadmaps being as unclear as they are, a lot can change between now and even the second half of the year when Diamond Rapids was originally supposed to launch. In contrast, AMD's EPYC Venice and Verano lineups seem remarkably stable, with the former on track for launch later this year and the latter in 2027.You'll notice we didn't talk about the Arc Xe graphics mentioned in the leak — that's because we've already covered that in detail in another post. With all the focus on data centers and AI, consumer products such as dedicated gaming GPUs keep getting sidelined, but at least the CPU side of things for the mainstream segment isn't as affected. Nonetheless, it's shaping up to be a fierce server battle between the Red and Blue Teams in the coming years. history.titleZh: 英特尔即将推出的Xeon 7“Diamond Rapids”服务器CPU据称推迟至2027年——下一代Coral Rapids系列将于2028年问世，但据新泄露消息，可加快进度
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔最新的服务器CPU路线图出现重大调整。原计划今年晚些时候推出的Diamond Rapids（钻石激流）家族中，8通道内存版本已被取消，仅保留16通道SKU，且整体发布时间推迟至2027年。这意味着英特尔将无法用该系列直接对抗AMD计划于2026年推出的EPYC Venice处理器。

根据爆料者Jaykihn的信息，Diamond Rapids首发将提供256个核心（全部为性能核，即P核），采用“Panther Cove-X”架构，随后几个月内还将推出512核版本。两者均支持16通道内存，借助MRDIMM 2技术可实现高达1.6 TB/s的内存带宽。这两款产品均兼容新的LGA9324插槽。值得注意的是，Diamond Rapids将是英特尔最后一代不支持超线程的至强处理器——其继任者Coral Rapids（珊瑚激流）将重新引入同步多线程（SMT）技术。

Coral Rapids计划于2028年中期推出，初期提供8通道内存版本。英特尔在最近的季度财报电话会议中表示，可根据市场需求加速或提前该产品的上市节奏。目前尚缺乏关于Coral Rapids的具体规格细节。

在Diamond Rapids之前，英特尔还将推出Clearwater Forest（清水森林），作为现有Sierra Forest（仅含能效核E核）的继任者，定位为Xeon 6+平台，旨在填补Xeon 6与Xeon 7（Diamond Rapids）之间的空白。它采用Intel 18A工艺制造，配备最多288个Darkmont E核，支持12通道DDR5-8000内存，预计于2026年上半年发布。

此外，泄露信息还提到了Crescent Island（新月岛）及Crescent Island工作站版本（2026年底，Xe3p架构）、Jaguar Shores（美洲虎海岸，2027年底，Xe4架构）等AI加速器产品的规划。

需要指出的是，上述大部分信息并非官方确认，英特尔当下的路线图仍存在较大不确定性，从目前到原定发布窗口（2026年下半年）之间可能发生诸多变化。相比之下，AMD的EPYC Venice（计划2026年）和Verano（计划2027年）产品线显得更为稳定。这场红蓝两队在服务器市场的竞争将在未来几年愈发激烈。

history.title: Maine governor vetoes bill that bans large new data centers — says legislature should’ve exempted one particular well-supported data center
history.description: Governor Janet Mills vetoed a bill that would have enacted a moratorium on all data center projects because it would affect one site to be built on a former paper mill in Jay, Franklin County.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Maine Governor Janet Mills just vetoed a bill that would ban all new data center projects in the state. This moratorium applies to all data centers that exceed 20MW of consumption and would have halted all approvals until October 2027, while a state council studies their impact on the local power and water supply, as well as their effect on surrounding air quality. According to Bangor Daily News, the governor said she supported the temporary moratorium on this power-hungry infrastructure if the legislature exempted a site that’s already under construction in Jay, a town in Franklin County that sits around 60 miles north of Portland.“A moratorium is appropriate given the impacts of massive data centers in other states on the environment and on electricity rates,” the Democrat governor said in a statement. “But the final version of this bill fails to allow for a specific project in [Jay] that enjoys strong local support from its host community and region.”The site where the Jay data center is being built was once a paper mill that suffered a catastrophic accident, leading to its closure in 2023. It’s reported that the $550 million project will use existing infrastructure, limiting its impact on the electric grid and energy bills, while also contributing to Jay’s property tax revenue. Furthermore, it’s expected to create 800 construction jobs and open 100 additional high-paying permanent positions once the data center begins operations.Article continues below You may like Virginia voter support for new data centers collapses from 69% in 2023 to 35% in new poll Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center It’s reported that the Democratic governor has never had a veto overturned by the legislature, which is led by the same party. However, the Maine House of Representatives voted against the exemption, 115-29, which is more than enough to meet the two-thirds majority required to override Mills. Aside from disagreeing with the majority of the Maine Democratic Party, this also exposes her to a political challenge as she attempts to secure a nomination as the Democratic contender for a U.S. Senate seat, facing political newcomer Graham Platner.Despite her move to block the bill, Mills said she will issue an executive order to create a state council to study the impact of data center projects in the state. She also signed a bill that put into law a measure preventing data center projects from taking advantage of the state’s business development tax incentive programs.Residents and local governments have started pushing back against data center projects after many communities with nearby infrastructure have reported increased electricity costs and poor power quality. It has gotten so bad that President Donald Trump convened some of the biggest hyperscalers at the White House and made them promise to “pay their own way” for their electricity needs. history.titleZh: 缅因州州长否决禁止新建大型数据中心的法案——称立法机构本应豁免一个特别受支持的数据中心
history.descriptionZh:
缅因州州长珍妮特·米尔斯（Janet Mills）近日否决了一项旨在暂停该州所有新建大型数据中心项目的法案。该法案原本计划对功耗超过20兆瓦的数据中心实施临时禁令，直至2027年10月，期间由州议会委员会研究这些设施对当地电力供应、水资源以及空气质量的影响。然而，米尔斯州长在否决声中提出了一个关键条件：她支持这项针对高能耗基础设施的临时禁令，但前提是立法机构必须豁免一个已经在富兰克林县杰伊镇（Jay）开工建设的项目——该镇位于波特兰以北约60英里处，原址曾是一家纸厂，2023年因重大事故关闭。

米尔斯在声明中解释了她的立场：“鉴于其他州大型数据中心对环境和电价的冲击，暂停是合理的。但该法案的最终版本未能为一个在杰伊镇获得当地社区和地区强烈支持的具体项目留出豁免空间。”据报道，这个耗资5.5亿美元的杰伊数据中心项目将利用现有基础设施，从而减少对电网和能源账单的负担，同时还能为杰伊镇增加房产税收入。此外，项目建设期间预计创造800个建筑岗位，运营后将开放100个高薪永久职位。

这一政治博弈的背景颇为复杂。米尔斯作为民主党籍州长，此前从未被同样由民主党主导的立法机构推翻过否决。然而，这次缅因州众议院以115票对29票的压倒性结果否决了豁免条款，这一票数远超推翻州长否决所需的三分之二多数。这意味着，如果立法机构后续正式投票推翻州长对整个法案的否决，米尔斯将面临政治尴尬。更关键的是，她正在争取民主党联邦参议员提名，而她的党内初选对手、政治新人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（Graham Platner）正可以利用这一分歧发起挑战。

尽管否决了法案，米尔斯表示将发布行政令，成立一个州级委员会研究数据中心项目的影响。她还签署了另一项法案，正式禁止数据中心项目利用该州的商业发展税收激励计划。这一系列动作反映出数据中心扩张引发的社会矛盾日益尖锐：许多社区报告称，附近大型数据中心导致居民电价上涨、供电质量恶化，民众和地方政府开始强烈抵制。甚至总统唐纳德·特朗普也曾召集几家最大的超大规模云服务商在白宫会面，要求他们必须“自掏腰包”解决电力需求问题。

history.title: Intel has reportedly cancelled discrete gaming GPUs for the upcoming Xe3P Arc "Celestial" family — gaming GPU remains uncertain even for the next-gen Xe4 "Druid" lineup that lands in 2027
history.description: New leaks claim that Intel's upcoming Xe3P graphics architecture won't feature any discrete gaming GPUs, and even the next-gen Xe4 lineup isn't confirmed to. Intel is instead prioritizing the datacenter and workstation segments for new graphics IP, and is featuring them on mobile parts.

Subscribe to our newsletter Panther Lake debuted Intel's latest Xe3 graphics architecture, but the IP isn't said to expand beyond iGPUs. For a while, we've known that "Xe3P" (codenamed Celestial) will serve as the refined follow-up, with many awaiting a proper successor to the existing Arc B-series dedicated GPUs. Unfortunately, a massive new leak dump from reliable tipster Jaykihn claims Intel canceled discrete gaming GPUs for Celestial a long time ago, and their fate hangs in the balance for even the next-gen Xe4 "Druid" architecture.Celestial was canned long ago.Druid is up in the air.April 24, 2026Originally, Xe3 was supposed to be Celestial with a planned 2025 launch, but Intel changed it to Battlemage, pushing Xe3P to be Celestial instead. That's why the iGPUs inside Panther Lake chips are classified as Battlemage parts. Last year, Intel announced the Crescent Island data center GPU as the first Xe3P/Celestial product, featuring an unprecedented 160 GB of LPDDR5X VRAM, slated for a 2026 release (that Jaykihn confirms for late 2026). The updated Intel Arc roadmap (Xe3 announced in 2025 but launched in 2026) (Image credit: Intel)Xe3P is also reportedly used across Nova Lake for the display and media engine block, while a special NVL-S desktop CPU featuring 12 Xe3P cores (iGPU) is also in the works; NVL-S otherwise features only 2 Xe cores. Nova Lake-H (mobile) is rumored to use Xe3P throughout the entire lineup. So that, mixed with Cresent Island, perhaps Arc Pro-series workstation GPUs, and the rumored "Razor Lake-AX" (Strix Halo competitor after Nova Lake-AX was canned), constitutes the entirety of Xe3P.Article continues below You may like Intel's Nova Lake lineup to feature a SKU with 12 Xe3P iGPU cores, compete with Ryzen G-series APUs Panther Lake for handhelds reportedly delayed into Q2 2026, Core G3 series will tackle AMD's Ryzen Z2 Intel's upcoming Xeon 7 "Diamond Rapids" server CPUs reportedly delayed to 2027 There is no discrete gaming-focused graphics card planned. That means Battlemage will remain Intel's latest gaming dGPU at least until the next-gen Xe4/Druid architecture comes along. Jaykihn's leak points to a late-2027 release for Druid, but the possibility of a discrete gaming GPU remains "up in the air." Intel is planning to focus on the AI sector once again, with "Jaguar Shores" marking the debut of Xe4.Snapshot, subject to change.Clearwater Forest 1H 2026.Diamond Rapids mid 2027, 16CH.Coral Rapids mid 2028, starting with 8CH.As mentioned in Q1 call, may be accelerated.Crescent Island and Crescent Island Workstation late 2026, Xe3p.Jaguar Shores late 2027, Xe4.April 24, 2026Around the same time, the "Titan Lake" mainstream consumer CPUs might also feature Xe4 IP for integrated graphics and/or other display blocks. A supposed "Arc D-series" for gamers yearning for a next-gen dedicated GPU from Intel remains unconfirmed. The successor to Xe4 is rumored to be planned for mid-to-late 2028, according to the leak, and is likely to debut on whatever comes after Jaguar Shores in the rack-scale segment.Xe3P = Celestial.Followed by Xe4 down the line.Xe4 successor may or may not be Xe5.Intel’s graphical architecture naming is not as straightforward as integer iterations; Xe4 itself has had multiple names, and multiple architectures have been referred to as Xe4, over time.April 24, 2026All of this information is, of course, subject to change and of a speculative nature. Intel's roadmaps haven't been the most consistent, so take these leaks with a grain of salt. The main takeaway is how Intel has almost given up on the discrete gaming GPU market after a turbulent launch for Alchemist and Battlemage. Given the AI boom we're in, it's easy to understand how Celestial production could've been entirely reallocated to serve the datacenter and workstation markets. Let's hope that Druid marks the revival of dedicated gaming GPUs from the Blue Team. history.titleZh: 据报道，英特尔已取消即将推出的Xe3P Arc "Celestial"系列独立游戏GPU — 即使是2027年登场的下一代Xe4 "Druid"系列，游戏GPU仍存变数
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔最新GPU路线图曝光：独立游戏显卡计划搁浅，重心转向AI与数据中心

近日，可靠爆料人Jaykihn泄露了英特尔GPU架构的详细路线图，揭示了代号为“Celestial”（Xe3P）和“Druid”（Xe4）的后续发展计划。消息称，英特尔已长期取消Celestial架构的独立游戏显卡项目，下一代Xe4“Druid”架构的独立游戏显卡命运同样悬而未决。

背景与架构演变

此前已知，Panther Lake处理器首次集成了英特尔最新的Xe3图形架构，但该IP仅限于集成GPU（iGPU），并未扩展至独立显卡。随后，代号“Celestial”的Xe3P架构被视为Xe3的改进版本，原本被寄望于成为现有Arc B系列独立游戏显卡的继任者。然而，根据最新泄露信息，英特尔在很久以前就砍掉了Celestial的独立游戏GPU计划。

架构命名上存在一定混淆：原计划中Xe3应是Celestial，预计2025年发布，但英特尔将其改为Battlemage（即当前Arc B系列），而Xe3P则成为Celestial。这也是为什么Panther Lake芯片中的iGPU被归类为Battlemage部件的原因。

Xe3P/Celestial的实际用途

虽然独立游戏显卡被取消，但Xe3P并未消失。英特尔已于去年宣布了Crescent Island数据中心GPU作为首个Xe3P/Celestial产品，配备前所未有的160GB LPDDR5X显存，计划于2026年下半年发布（Jaykihn确认时间为2026年底）。此外，Xe3P还将用于Nova Lake的显示与媒体引擎模块，一款配备12个Xe3P核心（iGPU）的NVL-S桌面CPU也在开发中（其他NVL-S型号仅2个Xe核心）。移动版Nova Lake-H则预计全系采用Xe3P。加上Crescent Island、可能的Arc Pro系列工作站显卡，以及传闻中取代被砍的Nova Lake-AX的“Razor Lake-AX”（对标Strix Halo），这些构成了Xe3P的全部阵容——没有任何面向游戏玩家的独立显卡产品。

Xe4/Druid的未来

这意味着Battlemage将至少在2027年底前保持英特尔最新的游戏独立GPU地位。Jaykihn的泄露显示，Xe4/Druid架构预计于2027年底发布，但独立游戏显卡的可能性仍“悬而未决”。英特尔正再次将重点转向AI领域，首个Xe4产品将是名为“Jaguar Shores”的数据中心/机架级GPU。同时，主流消费级CPU“Titan Lake”也可能集成Xe4用于iGPU或显示模块。而传说中的“Arc D系列”独立游戏显卡仍未得到确认。

泄露的后续路线图还包括：Xe4的继任者（可能叫Xe5或其它名称）计划于2028年中后期推出，预计首发于Jaguar Shores之后的机架级产品。

性能与市场影响

英特尔图形架构的命名并非简单的整数迭代，Xe4本身曾有过多个名称，多个架构也一度被统称为Xe4。此次泄露信息虽属推测且可能变动，但核心信号明确：在经历了Alchemist和Battlemage的波折后，英特尔几乎放弃了独立游戏GPU市场。在当前AI热潮下，不难理解为何Celestial的生产资源被完全重新分配给数据中心和工作站市场。对于玩家而言，希望或许只能寄托于Druid架构能带来蓝色阵营独立游戏显卡的复兴。

总结来看，英特尔GPU路线图已清晰转向：独立游戏显卡短期内不再有新品，未来数年重心将放在集成GPU、数据中心AI加速与工作站领域。Druid是否能成为游戏玩家期待的转折点，尚需时间验证。

history.title: Intel VP claims up to 30% of CPU performance is untapped by modern games — software optimization is critical to unlocking full potential of hybrid CPUs
history.description: Intel is claiming that better software optimization for hybrid architecture CPUs can unlock up to 30% more performance without hardware upgrades. The silicon itself is very capable, but its true potential is held back by code that perhaps prioritizes more conventional silicon and, therefore, benefits from brute-force upgrades like extra L3 cache.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel switched to a hybrid architecture for its CPUs back in 2021 with Alder Lake, mixing performance and efficiency cores on the same package, similar to ARM-based chips. Since then, the company has iterated on the design slowly but steadily. And even though a "unified core" is in the works, for now, the hybrid architecture seems to have reached maturity from a hardware standpoint, according to Intel VP Robert Hallock.Hallock sat down with PC Games Hardware and, during the interview, blamed the software side of things for performance issues on hybrid chips. He was referring to the practice of disabling E-cores on modern Intel CPUs to get better performance; some people report higher FPS in games when playing only on the P-cores. Hallock's response to this was that "they are virtually identical in performance… it’s about 1% difference."Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptHe went on to explain how the early state of the Intel Thread Director back in the day contributed to better P-core-only performance. Windows' task scheduler is essentially blind without the Thread Director actually telling it which process is better suited to which core (hence the name "director"). With better optimization, even though the E-cores aren't as powerful, they still contribute their fair share to the overall operation. Even simple tools like Intel's APO can help in this regard.Article continues below You may like Intel’s Binary Optimization Tool tested and explained Geekbench investigates up to 30% jump with Intel's iBOT Intel announces Arrow Lake Refresh CPUs, claims 15% higher gaming performance and multi-threaded boost Beyond that, though, there were also issues with low ring bus frequencies with E-cores at the time that contributed to worse performance. With them enabled, even if your P-cores were ready to boost much higher, the interconnect would be bottlenecked to lower speeds because the E-cores on the same die just couldn't keep up. Intel has worked to more clearly decouple the core clusters in subsequent generations, like Raptor and Arrow Lake. Arrow Lake 200S Die Shot (Image credit: Tony Yu from Asus, via Bilibili)Hallock continued, saying that he truly believes "that the general PC gaming market and especially enthusiasts [...] are significantly underestimating the importance of software to the PC experience." Software optimization is the next frontier of efficiency, of extracting more performance from the same silicon, because the silicon itself is seemingly not the bottleneck.Things like the new binary optimization feature Intel has packed inside the Arrow Lake refresh chips are an example of this. Even though it doesn't work for most apps and games yet — Geekbench even flagged it — it's proof that tuning code can lead to better performance on the same hardware. Beyond the program, everything from the driver to even the BIOS adds its own overhead, leaving performance on the table."Yes, you can make the game faster with a faster piece of hardware, but there's always going to be 10, 20, 30% performance hidden behind the fact that that game was just not optimized for your CPU," claimed Hallock. AMD's solution to this problem has been rather simple: just add a lot of SRAM next to the cores, aka 3D V-cache, so that the CPU's L3 cache needs are met quickly, helping achieve higher FPS in games.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsNova Lake has something similar in the works with its bLLC (Big Last Level Cache), but that's still a hardware solution. The thought of up to 30% performance just waiting to be extracted through better software optimization is therefore not overstated. In a way, Hallock is pointing fingers at developers and engineers who have optimized for AMD's relatively conventional silicon first, which affects the true potential of Intel's hybrid architecture. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 英特尔副总裁声称现代游戏未能发掘高达30%的CPU性能——软件优化对于释放混合CPU的全部潜力至关重要
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔自2021年推出Alder Lake处理器以来，一直采用类似ARM芯片的混合架构，在同一芯片上集成高性能核心（P核）与高能效核心（E核）。此后，该公司对这一设计进行了稳步迭代。尽管“统一核心”正在研发中，但英特尔副总裁Robert Hallock在接受PC Games Hardware采访时表示，从硬件角度看，混合架构已达到成熟阶段，问题主要出在软件层面。

针对部分用户为了提升游戏性能而关闭E核的做法（即仅使用P核运行游戏），Hallock回应称，实际性能差异微乎其微，大约只有1%。他解释，早期英特尔的Thread Director（线程调度器）技术尚不成熟，导致Windows任务调度器无法准确判断哪个线程适合运行在哪种核心上，从而使得P核独占模式一度表现更好。但随着优化深入，E核虽然性能较弱，但仍能对整体运行贡献力量，甚至像Intel APO（二进制优化工具）这样简单的软件也能显著改善调度效率。

除了调度问题，Hallock还指出了早期混合架构的另一个瓶颈：环形总线频率。当E核启用时，即使P核可以超频到更高频率，但由于E核无法跟上，互联总线会被迫降低速度，从而限制整体性能。英特尔在后续的Raptor Lake和Arrow Lake等世代中，通过更清晰地解耦不同核心集群来缓解这一问题。

Hallock强调，PC游戏市场尤其是发烧友群体“严重低估了软件对PC体验的重要性”。他认为软件优化是提取更多性能的新前沿，因为硅片本身似乎不再是瓶颈。例如，英特尔在Arrow Lake Refresh芯片中集成的全新二进制优化功能，尽管目前尚未支持大多数应用和游戏（Geekbench甚至因此发出警告），但它证明了通过调整代码可以在同款硬件上获得更好的性能。从驱动程序到BIOS，每一层软件都会产生开销，导致性能损失。Hallock指出：“用更快的硬件可以让游戏运行更快，但总有10%、20%、30%的性能隐藏在那款游戏没有针对你的CPU进行优化这一事实上。”

AMD对此问题的解决方案相对直接：在核心旁增加大量SRAM（即3D V-Cache），快速满足CPU的L3缓存需求，从而在游戏中实现更高帧率。而英特尔未来的Nova Lake也计划引入类似的大末级缓存（bLLC），但这仍然是硬件方案。因此，通过更好的软件优化最多可释放30%性能的说法并非夸大其词。从某种意义上说，Hallock是在批评那些优先针对AMD相对传统的硅片进行优化的开发者和工程师，这限制了英特尔混合架构的真正潜力。

history.title: AMD's memory-boosting EXPO 1.2 is here, adds support for three Chinese memory vendors — performance gains could be muted until Zen 6
history.description: Third-party AMD developer and hardware leakers share details of the new AMD EXPO 1.2 technology arriving on AMD AM5 motherboards.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter AMD's long-awaited EXPO 1.2 has finally arrived on AM5 motherboards. The new and improved memory overclocking technology will allow vendors to release fast memory kits for AMD that rival the best RAM. Renowned third-party AMD utility developer 1usmus and hardware leaker chi11eddog have detailed the improvements in EXPO 1.2. However, according to the former, the most substantial benefit will likely be unlocked only with AMD's next-generation Zen 6 processors. The new version also adds support for three Chinese RAM vendors.One of AMD EXPO 1.2's enhancements appears to be support for module geometry. While 1usmus didn’t provide extensive details, module geometry refers to the arrangement and structure of memory chips on a memory module. The feature may be related to HUDIMMs, a recent innovation that suppresses one of the 32-bit sub-channels in DDR5 memory modules, aiming to offer a cost-effective solution to the ongoing memory shortage.AMD EXPO 1.2 introduces support for MRDIMMs (Multiplexed Rank Dual In-line Memory Modules), designed to deliver substantially higher bandwidth and greater memory capacity. These memory modules are for server and data center platforms, though, so they won't be available on consumer AM5 platforms.Article continues below You may like AMD's next-gen 'Medusa Halo' APUs to reportedly use LPDDR6 RAM, enabling 80% higher bandwidth AMD addressing crushing memory shortages with Ryzen 7 9850X3D CPU bundles China’s CXMT and YMTC to memory output Sadly, EXPO 1.2 still only partially supports CUDIMMs (Clocked Unbuffered Dual In-line Memory Modules) and CSODIMMs (Clocked Small Outline Dual In-line Memory Modules). These modules leverage a CKD (Client Clock Driver), a small IC that maintains signal integrity and stabilizes memory operation at higher frequencies. It's a key component to help DDR5 continue to scale the frequency ladder.However, EXPO 1.2 retains bypass mode for CUDIMMs, treating them as standard memory modules without fully leveraging the CKD's benefits. There is mention of CKD bypass modes, but we don't yet have any detailed information on them. According to 1usmus, the current AGESA 1.3.0.1 firmware does not yet fully support CUDIMMs. According to 1usmus, AMD is likely laying the groundwork for full CUDIMM support in preparation for the upcoming Ryzen 10000 processors (codenamed Olympic Ridge) that will leverage AMD's new Zen 6 execution cores.$4.99 thrift store GPU from r/pcmasterraceAMD has been making significant behind-the-scenes improvements on EXPO 1.2. One of the standout features introduced is the new Ultra Low Latency (ULL) mode. It reportedly reduces memory latency. Hardware leaker chi11eddog claims that enabling ULL mode can decrease memory latency by an impressive five to seven nanoseconds on a typical DDR5-6000 memory kit.Other improvements that EXPO 1.2 brings to the table are new fields for further fine-tuning memory that overclocking enthusiasts will appreciate. The new memory timings include tREFI, tRRDS, and tWR, along with options for VDDP voltage.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIn response to the ongoing global memory crisis, AMD has proactively expanded memory compatibility by adding support for three Chinese memory manufacturers: RAMXEED Limited Conexant, Rui Xuan (formerly known as Rei Zuan), and Fujitsu Synaptics. These brands are popular in China, but it's unlikely you'll see their memory kits in the Western markets. However, consumers who purchase Chinese memory kits from overseas via platforms such as AliExpress can expect full compatibility thanks to AMD EXPO 1.2.Asus has already started deploying beta firmware with early support for AMD EXPO 1.2 across its 800-series motherboard lineup. Many of the company's X870E and X870 motherboards can access the new firmware; however, the B850 motherboards still have to wait a little while longer. Other motherboard manufacturers will likely follow suit very soon.AMD EXPO 1.2 is a great sign of commitment to continue improving the AM5 platform. However, we won't get to experience its full potential until Zen 6 hits the market, which should be sometime between late 2026 and early 2027. history.titleZh: AMD的内存提升技术EXPO 1.2到来，新增支持三家中国内存厂商——性能提升可能要到Zen 6才会显现
history.descriptionZh:
AMD 长期备受期待的 EXPO 1.2 内存超频技术终于登陆 AM5 主板，这一升级将为 AMD 平台带来与顶级内存相媲美的高速内存套件。知名第三方 AMD 工具开发者 1usmus 和硬件爆料者 chi11eddog 详细披露了 EXPO 1.2 的多项改进。然而，据 1usmus 透露，该技术最重大的优势可能需要等到 AMD 下一代 Zen 6 处理器（也就是 Ryzen 10000 系列，代号 Olympic Ridge）问世后才能完全解锁，预计时间在 2026 年底至 2027 年初之间。

在核心技术原理方面，EXPO 1.2 新增了对“模块几何”（module geometry）的支持，这指的是内存芯片在内存模块上的排列与结构。该特性可能与 HUDIMMs 有关——这是近期的一项创新，通过抑制 DDR5 内存模块中两个 32 位子通道之一，旨在以更低成本缓解内存短缺问题。此外，EXPO 1.2 还引入了对 MRDIMMs（多路复用秩双列内存模块）的支持，这类模块专为服务器和数据中心平台设计，可提供大幅提升的带宽和更大容量，但不会用于消费级 AM5 平台。

对于面向消费市场的 CUDIMMs（带时钟驱动的无缓冲双列直插式内存模块）和 CSODIMMs（带时钟驱动的小外形双列直插式内存模块），EXPO 1.2 仅提供部分支持。这些模块依赖 CKD（客户端时钟驱动器）——一种小型集成电路，用于保持信号完整性并稳定高频内存运行。但 EXPO 1.2 仍保留了 CUDIMMs 的旁路模式，将其作为标准内存模块处理，未能充分利用 CKD 的优势。当前 AGESA 1.3.0.1 固件尚未完全支持 CUDIMMs，AMD 可能正在为未来 Zen 6 处理器提供完整支持做准备。

性能参数方面，EXPO 1.2 引入了一项突出特性——全新的超低延迟（ULL）模式。据 chi11eddog 透露，在典型的 DDR5-6000 内存套件上启用 ULL 模式可将内存延迟降低惊人的 5 至 7 纳秒。此外，该版本还新增了多个内存时序微调选项，包括 tREFI、tRRDS 和 tWR，以及 VDDP 电压调节项，这无疑会深受超频爱好者的欢迎。

在技术影响和应用场景上，为应对全球内存危机，AMD 主动扩展了内存兼容性，新增了对三家中国内存厂商的支持：RAMXEED Limited Conexant、Rui Xuan（原 Rei Zuan）和 Fujitsu Synaptics。这些品牌在中国市场颇受欢迎，但在西方市场可能难以见到。不过，消费者通过海外平台（如 AliExpress）购买的中国内存套件将获得充分的 EXPO 1.2 兼容性。华硕已率先在其 800 系列主板（包括 X870E 和 X870）上部署了支持 EXPO 1.2 的测试版固件，B850 主板仍需等待，其他主板厂商预计很快跟进。总体而言，AMD EXPO 1.2 彰显了 AMD 持续改进 AM5 平台的承诺，尽管其全部潜力要等到 Zen 6 处理器发布后才能释放。
